@dominicbrown8935 No, no they are not. The percentage of women in the military in general is around 17-19%. The percentage of women in special forces is 7-12%. And this is roughly consistent as an average across most places in the world where women are not treated like subhuman second-rate citizens.

You literally do not know how to fact-check yourself, do you? Sorry but you're just a bigot.

@dominicbrown8935 The portion of females in the military ranges from about 10-19% in most places around the world. The percentage of women in special forces is lower, around 7-12%, but nowhere near 0. Just because women aren't in the navy seals does not mean they aren't in special forces. They don't join the navy seals because navy seals have rigorous SWIMMING training; it has nothing to do with an inability to keep up with the combat training, it's just easier for a big guy to swim in turbulent ocean waters and that is an absolute requirement for their training. That and this was a branch of the military where women were not allowed in previous times, so there was no precedent for it and there's a big stigma of sexism deterring women from even wanting to join the navy seals. I know all of this as my father is a navy seal.

But, again, there are PLENTY of other special forces divisions. For instance, women currently make up 8% of the US Army Special Operations Forces, just as one of MANY examples.

And even if you were REMOTELY right, you're looking solely at the US military for some unfathomable reason. This is a German movie, about a German special forces soldier, and as of 2021 women have been known to enter into a variety of their special forces divisions. Their military works a bit different from the US military so things aren't categorized the same, but women make up 13% of the total German military and plenty of them and do indeed occupy positions in special forces units.

You just don't see or hear about any of this very frequently because women don't make up a huge portion of any military to begin with. This is due to a mix of women not being ALLOWED into most militaries until fairly recent times, and the lingering sexist culture around the military further pushing women away from considering such roles. And as a result, there are even less women in special forces. But to say there are none is unfathomably idiotic and EASILY fact-checked.

And again, even if anything you're trying to say were actually correct (it's not), it's a movie. The fact that you care so much about a female lead in an action movie says more about you than anything.
